coverage.evidenceText: Coherent is ramping internal InP output and 6-inch wafer demand for AI infrastructure.
coverage.notes: The sources describe internal InP output and wafer demand; no external wafer revenue, share, or growth is disclosed.

coverage.evidenceText: The 6-inch platform produces CW lasers alongside EMLs and photodiodes.
coverage.notes: Recorded as product/capability coverage only. The Q3 sources do not disclose standalone CW laser revenue, share, or growth.

coverage.evidenceText: The 6-inch platform produces EMLs alongside CW lasers and photodiodes.
coverage.notes: Recorded as product/capability coverage only. The Q3 sources do not disclose standalone EML revenue, share, or growth.

coverage.evidenceText: Multiple CPO architectures, Silicon Photonics, InP, and VCSEL enable flexible networks.
coverage.notes: VCSEL is named as a technology route, but the Q3 sources do not disclose VCSEL revenue, share, or growth.

coverage.evidenceText: The 6-inch platform produces photodiodes alongside EMLs and CW lasers.
coverage.notes: This generic PD node is used because the source says photodiodes but does not specify silicon integrated PD or EML/DFB PD module revenue.

coverage.evidenceText: The 6-inch platform produces photodiodes, and Silicon Photonics is listed among flexible network technologies.
coverage.notes: Photodiode capability is named, but no standalone photodiode revenue, share, or growth is disclosed.

coverage.evidenceText: The presentation lists CPO/NPO as new revenue in H2 2026.
coverage.notes: Recorded as roadmap coverage. Q3 FY2026 does not disclose current NPO revenue or growth.

coverage.evidenceText: Multiple CPO architectures, Silicon Photonics, InP, and VCSEL enable flexible networks.
coverage.notes: Silicon Photonics is named as a technology route; the sources do not isolate modulator revenue or current commercialization.

coverage.evidenceText: Coherent describes end-to-end optical integration for next-generation AI architectures and lists CPO/NPO as new revenue in H2 2026.
coverage.notes: Recorded as roadmap/capability coverage. Q3 FY2026 does not disclose silicon photonic engine revenue or growth.

coverage.evidenceText: The presentation names multiple CPO architectures and lists CPO/NPO as new revenue in H2 2026.
coverage.notes: Not treated as current Q3 FY2026 external sales because the source places CPO/NPO new revenue in H2 2026.

coverage.evidenceText: The Datacenter & Communications segment includes transceivers and modules; the presentation lists transceivers as growth engines.
coverage.notes: Broad optical module parent node used because the sources do not split revenue by EML/DFB versus silicon photonic module route.

financial.attributionEvidenceText: The segment includes transceivers and modules, and management attributed growth to strong datacenter and communications demand.
financial.notes: Use D&C segment growth as a broad proxy. No individual optical module revenue, share, or growth is disclosed.

coverage.evidenceText: The Datacenter & Communications segment includes transceivers and modules; transceivers at 1.6T, 3.2T, and 6.4T are listed as growth engines.
coverage.notes: Required optical_module child. Kept as route coverage only because the sources do not identify the transceiver route as EML/DFB or disclose EML/DFB module financials.

coverage.evidenceText: The segment includes transceivers and modules, and the presentation names Silicon Photonics among technologies for flexible networks.
coverage.notes: Required optical_module child. Kept as route coverage only because Silicon Photonics is named as a technology route, but no silicon photonic pluggable module financial split is disclosed.

coverage.evidenceText: The investor presentation lists DCI Transceivers ZR/ZR+ as growth engines.
coverage.notes: Recorded as product-line coverage only. The Q3 sources do not disclose DCI/ZR revenue, share, or growth.

coverage.evidenceText: The investor presentation lists Optical Circuit Switch as a growth engine.
coverage.notes: Recorded as product-line coverage only. The Q3 sources do not disclose OCS revenue, share, or growth.

downstreamAttributions.attributionEvidenceText: Management said AI datacenter infrastructure continues to scale and that Coherent is expanding capacity to meet demand.
downstreamAttributions.notes: AI infrastructure is the clearest demand driver, but the sources do not disclose revenue by AI server attachment.

downstreamAttributions.attributionEvidenceText: The presentation names optical networking products, 400G/lane links, optical circuit switch, transceivers, and multi-rail for AI datacenters.
downstreamAttributions.notes: Mapped to the data center networking side of the topology. No switch-specific revenue or growth is disclosed.

downstreamAttributions.attributionEvidenceText: The sources repeatedly attribute demand to AI datacenter infrastructure and scalable high-performance infrastructure.
downstreamAttributions.notes: Used as terminal demand context. Product nodes do not directly auto-generate edges to cloud_dc in the taxonomy.

qualitativeSignals.evidenceText: Management said the quarter was driven by exceptionally strong demand across datacenter and communications, and that AI datacenter infrastructure scaling is driving capacity expansion.
qualitativeSignals.notes: This is a qualitative demand signal only; it should not change YoY line width because no AI-server-specific revenue split is disclosed.

qualitativeSignals.evidenceText: Coherent says it is ramping Datacenter and Communications capacity end to end, is on track to double internal InP output by year-end and more than double again by 2027, and produces EMLs, CW lasers, and photodiodes on a 6-inch platform.
qualitativeSignals.notes: Targets internal capacity and component capability, not standalone external revenue.

qualitativeSignals.evidenceText: The 10-Q says Coherent entered into a multi-year capacity agreement with NVIDIA that may require incremental investments in equipment, labor, and working capital to support future production volumes through 2030.
qualitativeSignals.notes: The source does not name a specific product in the agreement, so the optical module and AI server targets are contextual to the report's datacenter demand narrative.

qualitativeSignals.evidenceText: The presentation lists CPO/NPO as new revenue in H2 2026.
qualitativeSignals.notes: Roadmap signal only. It is not treated as current Q3 FY2026 revenue.

qualitativeSignals.evidenceText: The presentation lists transceivers at 1.6T, 3.2T, and 6.4T, DCI transceivers, transport/transmission, optical circuit switch, optical components, and multi-rail as growth engines.
qualitativeSignals.notes: These are named growth engines without individual revenue or growth disclosure.
